chapter_title.ar: باب أَدْنَى مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنَ التَّسْبِيحِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ وَ السُّجُودِ وَ أَكْثَرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The least of what would suffice from the Glorification (Tasbīḥ) during the Rukū  and the Sajdah, and the most of it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/142)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/142)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي فَعَدَدْتُ لَهُ فِي الرُّكُوعِ وَ السُّجُودِ سِتِّينَ تَسْبِيحَةً .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ahmad Bin Umar Al Halby, from his father, from Aban Bin Taghlub who said,
‘I went over to Abu Abdullahasws and heasws was praying Salāt. So I numbered for himasws saying during the Rukū and the Sajdah, sixty Glorifications’.
en.sarwar: 2. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Amir from Ali ibn Mahziyar from ibn Faddal from Ahmad ibn ‘Umar al-Halabiy from his father from Aban ibn Taghlib who has said the following: “I once went to visit abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, when he performed Salah (prayer). I counted his Tasbih in Ruku’ (bowing down on one’s knees) and Sujud (prostration) which were sixty Tasbih.’”
